title: Dust raises $40M Series B to scale multiplayer AI for human-agent collaboration | Dust Blog
full_text: Dust raises $40M Series B to scale multiplayer AI for human-agent collaboration | Dust Blog NewDust announces Series B to fuel next chapter of growth Today, Dust is used by more than 3,000 organizations globally, from high-growth AI-native companies to established enterprises. Across those companies, the people closest to the work have deployed more than 300,000 agents. At Clay, Dust serves as foundational knowledge infrastructure for a rapidly growing GTM team. At Profound, it has become the source of truth for customer intelligence and post-sales. At Persona, teams across 11 departments have deployed more than 300 Dust agents. And at Doctolib, Dust is central to a company-wide AI strategy reaching 3,000 employees. We call this multiplayer AI. Today, we’re announcing a $40M Series B with Abstract, Sequoia, Snowflake Ventures, and Datadog to accelerate building it. Admins get complete visibility and control over every agent interaction. That means granular permissions, cost and usage monitoring, a full audit trail, and agent analytics, all in one place. Dust is also SOC 2 Type II certified, GDPR compliant with EU/US data residency, and guarantees zero model training on your data. If you think about the most complex work that happens at companies today, it’s not being done by just one person in isolation. Launching a product, onboarding a customer, conducting deal reviews, and running competitive teardowns can each involve dozens of humans and agents working in parallel sometimes over weeks and months — researching, synthesizing information, generating deliverables, coordinating follow-ups, and taking action from the same live context. announcement, Stan and I worked with our marketing, data, and operations teams together in Dust, running parallel workstreams with multiple people and agents contributing simultaneously. While humans made decisions, refined messaging, and coordinated approvals, agents synthesized data and updates from Slack, Snowflake, HubSpot, Notion, Gmail, and Google Drive. Our marketing team collaborated with agents to draft this blog post, prepare investor quote sheets, generate social copy, and build customer communication kits. Our data team's agents pulled the metrics you'll read below, live from our data warehouse. Our ops team coordinated the launch timeline, media embargo schedule, and approval workflows, with agents flagging blockers and @mentioning the right person when a decision was needed. Developer Platform Dust for Engineers Platform Documentation Github Repo / score=78.917 / query=AI Agent funding enterprise customers / intent=find_startups / path=official_original

## clean_text

Dust raises $40M Series B to scale multiplayer AI for human-agent collaboration | Dust Blog NewDust announces Series B to fuel next chapter of growth Today, Dust is used by more than 3,000 organizations globally, from high-growth AI-native companies to established enterprises. Across those companies, the people closest to the work have deployed more than 300,000 agents. At Clay, Dust serves as foundational knowledge infrastructure for a rapidly growing GTM team. At Profound, it has become the source of truth for customer intelligence and post-sales. At Persona, teams across 11 departments have deployed more than 300 Dust agents. And at Doctolib, Dust is central to a company-wide AI strategy reaching 3,000 employees. We call this multiplayer AI. Today, we’re announcing a $40M Series B with Abstract, Sequoia, Snowflake Ventures, and Datadog to accelerate building it. Admins get complete visibility and control over every agent interaction. That means granular permissions, cost and usage monitoring, a full audit trail, and agent analytics, all in one place. Dust is also SOC 2 Type II certified, GDPR compliant with EU/US data residency, and guarantees zero model training on your data. If you think about the most complex work that happens at companies today, it’s not being done by just one person in isolation. Launching a product, onboarding a customer, conducting deal reviews, and running competitive teardowns can each involve dozens of humans and agents working in parallel sometimes over weeks and months — researching, synthesizing information, generating deliverables, coordinating follow-ups, and taking action from the same live context. announcement, Stan and I worked with our marketing, data, and operations teams together in Dust, running parallel workstreams with multiple people and agents contributing simultaneously. While humans made decisions, refined messaging, and coordinated approvals, agents synthesized data and updates from Slack, Snowflake, HubSpot, Notion, Gmail, and Google Drive. Our marketing team collaborated with agents to draft this blog post, prepare investor quote sheets, generate social copy, and build customer communication kits. Our data team's agents pulled the metrics you'll read below, live from our data warehouse. Our ops team coordinated the launch timeline, media embargo schedule, and approval workflows, with agents flagging blockers and @mentioning the right person when a decision was needed.
